creator: jhixson@FreeBSD.org
text:
This is ready to commit. Have we figured out of the field is correct or not? (In reply to Mark Johnston from comment #11)

I created this port about a year ago. At that point security/sssd was at least 5 years out of date. I'm sure there are some users but I can't imagine many. 

Looking at sys/fs/procfs/procfs_status.c in procfs_doprocstatus():

/* comm pid ppid pgid sid tty ctty,sldr start ut st wmsg
                                euid ruid rgid,egid,groups[1 .. ngroups]
*/

So it seems the 12th field is the correct field. 

I've tested that the patch compiles but have not tested the functionality. I am interested in seeing the sysctl method of doing this rather than relying on procfs or linprocfs. What do y'all think?
